name: September
description: Labor Day, the equinox, the Constitution, 9/11, and the month named after seven.

questions.q: How many days does September have?

source.notes: Per the rhyme: 'Thirty days hath September, April, June, and November.'

questions.q: From which Latin numerical prefix does September get its name?

source.notes: September was originally the seventh month.

questions.q: Which U.S. holiday on the first Monday of September honors workers?
On what date in 1787 was the U.S. Constitution signed in Philadelphia?
On what date did the 9/11 terrorist attacks occur?
Who was the U.S. president on September 11, 2001?
Which U.S. president was assassinated on September 6, 1901?

source.notes: Shot at the Pan-American Exposition in Buffalo; died Sept 14. Theodore Roosevelt became president.

questions.q: What is September's traditional birthstone?
What is September's birth flower?

source.notes: Aster and morning glory are both common; aster is the most-cited birth flower.

questions.q: What is the popular name for the September full moon?

source.notes: The full moon nearest the autumn equinox; also called the Corn Moon.

questions.q: Around what date does the autumn (September) equinox typically fall?

source.notes: Marks the start of astronomical autumn in the Northern Hemisphere (and spring in the Southern).

questions.q: Which best-selling horror novelist was born on September 21, 1947?
Which Jewish holiday, the 'Jewish New Year', falls in September or early October?

source.notes: Determined by the Hebrew calendar; usually falls in September or early October.

questions.q: Which Asian harvest celebration, also called the Moon Festival, falls in September or October?

source.notes: Especially big in China, Vietnam, and Korea (as Chuseok). Mooncakes are the iconic food.

questions.q: Which UN observance promotes a global ceasefire on September 21?
Which U.S. president pardoned Richard Nixon in September 1974, weeks after taking office?
On what September date in 1939 did World War II officially begin with Germany's invasion of Poland?

source.notes: Britain and France declared war on Germany on Sept 3.

questions.q: What season begins in the Southern Hemisphere on the September equinox?

source.notes: September equinox = vernal (spring) equinox in the Southern Hemisphere.

questions.q: Who is the only British monarch to have died in September of the 21st century, after the longest reign in British history?

source.notes: Died September 8, 2022, after 70 years on the throne.

questions.q: Which August 1939 letter from Albert Einstein to FDR warned of German atomic-bomb research?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: removed inaccurate 'received in September' phrasing — the letter was signed Aug 2, 1939 but Alexander Sachs did not deliver it to Roosevelt until Oct 11, 1939. Helped trigger the U.S. Manhattan Project.
